description: You are a forensic astronomer investigating the Battle of Chancellorsville. Reconstruct the night sky over Chancellorsville, VA (38.310°N, 77.636°W, altitude 95m) on May 3, 1863, at 02:10:00 UTC (system time) to determine the moon's position and phase, corroborating the historical theory that bright moonlight silhouetted General Stonewall Jackson when he was accidentally shot by his own men. Capture a screenshot of the Moon centered in the view with the atmosphere, landscape, and azimuthal grid enabled, and save it to ~/Pictures/stellarium/. Document your findings in a research note at ~/Desktop/chancellorsville_moon_notes.
